message:
feat(membrane): add p-adic valuation monitor, symplectic reduction controller, and Stdlib API family

Extends the runtime math kernel with two new alien-artifact monitors and
a ninth API family enum variant:

1. padic_valuation.rs (583 lines): Non-Archimedean regime drift detector
   for floating-point exponent structures. Tracks 2-adic valuations of
   observed risk metrics to detect numerical regime transitions (normal →
   denormal drift → exceptional regime with NaN/Inf/subnormal floods)
   that are invisible to standard Archimedean statistical analysis.
   Implements math stack item: "non-Archimedean valuation bounds +
   interval-certified envelopes" from Round R11 (soft-fp/fenv).

2. symplectic_reduction.rs (540 lines): Resource lifecycle admissibility
   controller based on symplectic geometry. Models shared-memory segments,
   semaphores, message queues, and file descriptors as phase-space
   coordinates with Hamiltonian energy conservation constraints. Detects
   when resource acquisition/release trajectories leave the admissible
   polytope (capacity violations, deadlock-prone trajectories). Implements
   math stack item: "symplectic reduction + integer-lattice admissibility
   polytopes" from Round R29 (SysV IPC lifecycle semantics).

3. ApiFamily::Stdlib variant (index 8, COUNT bumped to 9): Enables
   per-family risk tracking, e-process alarming, and bandit routing
   for stdlib operations (atoi, strtol, qsort, exit, etc.) which were
   previously unclassified.

Runtime integration in RuntimeMathKernel::decide():
- p-adic state contributes risk bonus: ExceptionalRegime +110k ppm,
  DenormalDrift +45k ppm (calibrated to match spectral/persistence tiers).
- Symplectic state contributes risk bonus: Inadmissible +120k ppm,
  NearBoundary +50k ppm (highest single-monitor bonus, reflecting
  deadlock severity).
- Both monitors fed during observe() on every ABI call, with cached
  atomic state for lock-free hot-path reads.

Snapshot reporting extended with padic_ultrametric_distance,
padic_drift_count, symplectic_energy, symplectic_violation_count.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
